evals.prompt: My active-skills directory has grown a lot. Review all of the skills and tell me if any of them can be consolidated.
evals.expected_output: The agent runs scripts/portfolio_scan.py across the whole directory, groups skills into candidate clusters, applies the umbrella-class test to each, and produces a report recommending which clusters to consolidate (and how) versus keep as-is — without destructively merging before approval.
evals.trap: Without the skill, the agent eyeballs a few skill names, guesses at overlaps pairwise, and never applies the umbrella-class test, checks package integrity, or plans reference migration.

assertions.description: Runs portfolio_scan.py across the full portfolio rather than comparing a couple of skills by hand.
Applies the umbrella-class test per cluster and names the shared class, not just 'these are similar'.
Recommends a mechanism (merge existing / create umbrella / demote) per consolidation and keeps distinct skills separate.
Does not archive/merge skills before presenting the plan and getting approval.

evals.prompt: Consolidate git-sync and refresh-skills if they belong together, and make sure nothing else references the one you archive.
evals.expected_output: The agent confirms the umbrella class, picks the umbrella, checks package integrity (support files, skill_md_links), archives the absorbed sibling's whole package to .archive/, and migrates every inbound reference (sibling SKILL.md pointers, evals), then emits the consolidations block.
evals.trap: The agent flattens one SKILL.md into the other and leaves dangling references to the archived skill in sibling skills.

assertions.description: Checks package integrity before moving (support files and relative links).
Migrates inbound references (sibling SKILL.md pointers, eval prompts) to the umbrella.
Emits the structured consolidations/prunings summary recording the forwarding target.

evals.prompt: Review my SKILL.md in skills/gcloud and fix whatever isn't following best practices.
evals.expected_output: This is a near-miss. Reviewing/improving a SINGLE skill is skill-improvement's job, not a portfolio-wide consolidation review. This skill should not trigger.
evals.trap: Over-triggers on the word 'skill' / 'review' and runs a whole-portfolio scan when the user asked about one skill.

assertions.description: Does NOT invoke the portfolio consolidation workflow (defers to skill-improvement).
